title: malva parviflora subsp. oxyloba
intro: Malva parviflora subsp. oxyloba, also known as the small-flowered mallow, is a subspecies of the Malva parviflora plant, which is a member of the mallow family (Malvaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malva parviflora subsp. oxyloba include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
